meaning: stop working (a machine); or lose control of emotions
example:
My car broke down.
The talks broke down.
She broke down in tears.

Carry on with your work.
Carry on, don't stop.
They carried on talking.

I came across an old photo.
She came across as kind.
We came across a nice cafe.

meaning: finally be in a situation or place
example:
We ended up walking home.
He ended up a teacher.
You'll end up tired.

I can't figure it out.
Let's figure out a plan.
She figured out the code.

Fill in your name here.
Fill in the blanks.
I filled in the form.

She got over the flu.
He can't get over the loss.
Get over it!

He finally gave in.
Don't give in to pressure.
They refused to give in.

They went through a hard time.
Let's go through the list.
I went through your notes.

Hold on a second.
Hold on, I'm coming.
Please hold on.

meaning: continue at the same level or speed
example:
Keep up the good work.
I can't keep up.
Keep up with the news.

Don't let me down.
He let the team down.
I felt let down.

They made up after the argument.
He made up an excuse.
Let's make up.

She pointed out my mistake.
Let me point out one thing.
He pointed out the risks.

I can't put up with the noise.
She puts up with a lot.
Why put up with it?

We set off early.
They set off at dawn.
Let's set off now.

A new manager took over.
They took over the company.
Can you take over for me?

He turned up late.
Turn up the music.
She didn't turn up.

meaning: raise a child; or mention a topic
example:
She brought up a good point.
They brought up three kids.
Don't bring that up.

meaning: register at a hotel or airport
example:
We checked in at noon.
Check in two hours early.
I'll check in online.

meaning: leave a hotel; or look at something
example:
Check out this website.
We checked out at ten.
Check out the new menu.

Look up the word.
I looked up the address.
Look it up online.

Let's move on.
It's time to move on.
Move on to the next topic.

He didn't show up.
She showed up late.
Did anyone show up?

I'll sort out the problem.
Sort out your desk.
We sorted it out.

meaning: start a hobby; or occupy time or space
example:
I took up running.
It takes up too much space.
She took up painting.

Don't throw away the box.
He threw away the receipt.
Don't throw away your chance.

meaning: prepare your body; or become warmer
example:
Warm up before exercise.
The room warmed up.
Let's warm up first.

I'm cutting down on sugar.
Cut down your hours.
We cut down on costs.

I ran into an old friend.
We ran into trouble.
She ran into her teacher.

meaning: end a relationship; or separate into pieces
example:
They broke up last month.
The crowd broke up.
School breaks up in June.
